channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump envoy says Ukrainians 'brought it on themselves' after US pauses aid and intelligence sharing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ukraine was given “fair warning” by the White House before President Donald Trump this week ordered a pause on U.S. military assistance and intelligence sharing with Kyiv, a senior administration official said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: No prison time for man who bashed, disfigured mother’s face in front of child
item.description: Trevor Lee Colombano, 38, faced up to nine years in state prison in addition to 18 months in county jail after pleading no contest to assault with a deadly weapon, sexual battery and mayhem.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, NewsNation

item.title: Car being pulled from an Oregon river might have belonged to a family that vanished in 1958
item.description: CASCADE LOCKS, Ore. (AP) — Authorities said Thursday that they planned to pull a station wagon from an Oregon river believed to have belonged to a family of five that disappeared nearly 70 years ago while out searching for Christmas greenery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump signs tariff exemptions for certain imports from Canada, Mexico
item.description: President Trump on Thursday signed off on tariff exemptions for imports from Canada and Mexico that are covered under a 2020 North American trade agreement, marking the latest reversal of duties imposed earlier this week. Under the orders, goods compliant with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio State Highway Patrol supervisors punished after reported abuse
item.description: What began as an investigation into one man resulted in repercussions for two additional men in positions of power as the investigative process progressed.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Stone, NewsNation

item.title: California landlords prohibited from charging these fees starting in April
item.description: The bill also prohibits landlords or agents from charging a tenant any fee for paying rent or security deposits by check.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, NewsNation

item.title: At the Voice of America, the Trump administration is moving swiftly to assert its vision
item.description: As it has with other government agencies, the Trump administration is moving swiftly to assert its vision at the Voice of America.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Life-saving avalanche forecasts could be hindered by Trump budget cuts
item.description: LEADVILLE, Colo. (AP) — Officials in the Western U.S. who warn the public about avalanches are sounding a different type of alarm.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Elon Musk tells Republican lawmakers he's not to blame for the federal firings
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s not his fault. Billionaire Elon Musk is telling Republican lawmakers that he is not to blame for the firings of thousands of federal workers, including veterans, as pushes to downsize the government.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A fifth of US butterflies have disappeared since 2000: Study
item.description: Butterflies are disappearing from the United States at an alarming rate, with their total population declining by more than a fifth over two decades, a new study has found.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New Senate bill seeks to tackle the flow of fentanyl into the US from China and Mexico
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In a rare bipartisan move, two senators have introduced legislation in the latest attempt to fight the flow of fentanyl and its precursors into the United States from China, Mexico and through other borders — one reason that President Donald Trump has given for his new tariffs.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: CDC rehiring some laid-off workers
item.description: Administration officials told the CDC last month that nearly 1,300 of its probationary employees would be cut, but only approximately 700 to 750 employees ended up receiving termination notices.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KREX 5

item.title: California's Gavin Newsom opposes trans athletes in women's sports, splitting with progressives
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has spoken out against allowing transgender women and girls to compete in female sports.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Oregon man accused of throwing Molotov cocktails, shooting at Tesla dealership
item.description: Adam Matthew Lansky, 41, has been arraigned on federal charges after being accused of attempting to destroy property at a Tesla dealership twice.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, Fox 5

item.title: Roy Ayers,'Everybody Loves the Sunshine' musician, passes away at 84
item.description: Los Angeles native, Roy Ayers, who was behind the popular song "Everybody Loves the Sunshine" has passed away. He was 84 years old.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: Louisiana and Arkansas look to nitrogen executions
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Hours after the nation’s first execution by nitrogen gas in January last year, Alabama's attorney general urged other states to also develop it as a method for carrying out death sentences. Now, some states are following suit.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, KIM CHANDLER and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Migrants clone Border Patrol vehicles in bid to bypass Trump's crackdown
item.description: Some human smugglers have turned to cloning Border Patrol vehicles to bypass President Trump's crackdown on illegal border crossings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House hunting surges where LA wildfires raged: Report
item.description: As Los Angeles residents recover from wildfires, house-hunting in many of the communities affected by the fires is reportedly surging.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Alleged Abbey Gate plotter extradited to US to face 'justice for our 13,' FBI Director Kash Patel says
item.description: The mastermind behind the Abbey Gate bombing that killed scores of people, including 13 U.S. service members, has been brought back to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eric Adams faces calls to resign from Democrats at fiery House hearing on immigration
item.description: Embattled New York City Mayor Eric Adams (D) faced a barrage of criticism at a House Oversight and Accountability Committee hearing with so-called sanctuary city mayors on Wednesday — with much of the backlash coming from his fellow Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: AbbVie enters into almost $2B contract with Gubra for obsesity drug
item.description: Bloomberg reports the obesity drug market is expected to reach as much as $130 billion in annual sales by 2030.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Student group challenges Texas A&M drag ban
item.description: Texas A&M’s Board of Regents voted almost unanimously late last month in favor of a resolution that states drag events are inconsistent with the system’s “mission and core values.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: All My Friends Are Wrong
item.description: NewsNation Chief Washington Anchor and On Balance host Leland Vittert gives you an early look at tonight’s 7 p.m. ET show.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Florida jewelry heist suspect allegedly swallowed more than $750,000 in Tiffany & Co earrings, photo shows
item.description: A suspect allegedly swallowed more than $750,000 worth of Tiffany & Co. earrings following a jewelry heist at a shopping mall in Orlando, Florida, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California congressmember pushes Trump for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: A San Diego congressional representative is pressing the Norwegian committee who awards the prestigious Nobel Prizes to consider President Donald Trump to be the 2025 recipient of the peace prize.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, KTLA 5

item.title: Boy reportedly kidnapped from Atlanta 7 years ago found in Colorado; 2 arrested
item.description: A 14-year-old boy who went missing seven years ago has been located, some 1,400 miles from where he was allegedly abducted.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, ABC 4

item.title: Popular cartoon voice actor and comedian dead at 67
item.description: George Lowe, a voice actor and comedian best known for portraying Space Ghost on the partially animated TV series "Space Ghost Coast to Coast," has died. Lowe, 67, passed away on Sunday, a representative for the actor confirmed to outlets including Deadline and A.V. Club.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump passes Clinton, gives longest modern address to joint session of Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump gave the longest address to a joint session of Congress in modern history on Tuesday night, surpassing former President Bill Clinton’s previous mark.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Jimmy John's is giving out free sandwiches. Here's how to redeem the limited-time deal
item.description: Sandwich chain Jimmy John’s is giving away $1 million worth of toasted subs on Tuesday to celebrate a new product launch.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KRON4

item.title: Trump wants to use the 'God Squad' to increase logging, but it must follow strict rules
item.description: President Donald Trump wants to increase logging in national forests and on public lands, including by bypassing endangered species protections.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fargo has an election method that helps mainstream candidates. North Dakota lawmakers may ban it
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — When Fargo voters approved a unique local election system in 2018, their goal was to choose candidates with broader support to lead North Dakota’s largest city. It worked — maybe too well.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gerlinde Kaltenbrunner climbed the world's 14 highest peaks without oxygen or porters
item.description: Gerlinde Kaltenbrunner, an Austrian-born mountaineer, became the first woman to climb the world's 14 highest peaks without the help of oxygen or porters.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: 2 leaders of a group suspected of smuggling 20,000 immigrants are arrested in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Federal authorities in Los Angeles arrested two alleged leaders of a criminal organization suspected of smuggling 20,000 people from Guatemala to the U.S. and charging each person as much as $18,000 to get them into the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US stocks tumble after Trump announces Canadian and Mexican tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks are falling sharply Monday after President Donald Trump said tariffs he announced on Canada and Mexico will take effect on Tuesday. The bond market also took a hit following the latest warning signal on the strength of the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Magnitude 4.5 earthquake strikes in Washington state
item.description: A “notable” earthquake registering at a preliminary magnitude of 4.5 struck Washington State on Monday morning, according to the United States Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Kroger CEO resigns amid personal conduct investigation
item.description: McMullen's departure comes months after Kroger's failed two-year effort to merge with another supermarket giant, Albertsons.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, Fox 5

item.title: What to watch when Trump gives his big speech to Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump loves a good spectacle, and it's hard to top a speech to a joint session of Congress. The House chamber is packed with lawmakers, and the president's arrival is announced in a booming voice by the sergeant-at-arms, triggering cacophonous applause.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4
